parsed_review.summary: CP00-476 is a descriptor-only RP15.P08.M06 "Synthetic Fixture Set" slice (10 units, RP15.P08.M06.S08-S17, risk class A, deliverables hermes_evidence:3/claude_review:1/implementation:6). Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only: (1) ANALYTICS_CORE_CP476_PACK_BINDING / REQUIREMENTS / NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION in registry.js match the contract's current_pack, cp476_requirements, cp476_no_write_attestation, the closeout-pack-plan CP00-476 entry, and the manifest plan_binding_snapshot (unit count, range, distribution, section rows all agree). (2) service.js builders (createAnalyticsCoreCp476P08FixtureSliceCaseSet/Descriptor) emit frozen descriptor-only rows for all 10 required titles with descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false; freezeCp476Result stamps a complete no-write/no-real-data attestation. (3) validators.js coverage/slice/Hermes/Claude/handoff functions enforce unit/range/distribution, descriptor-only row shape, authority boundary, and handoff targets. (4) model.test.js and scripts/validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs assert coverage.valid, slice.valid, deepEqual of the contract p08_fixture_slice_descriptor / cp476_requirements / cp476_no_write_attestation against generated objects (drift guards), authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, and historical_pack_bindings tail = CP00-476. (5) Security sweep found no fs/network/child_process/process.env/secret usage in the changed analytics source or validator — the no-write/no-real-data boundary holds. (6) production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false: production gates are not weakened; only the descriptor-level flag analytics_core_p08_fixture_slice_descriptor_verified is asserted. (7) Authority boundary intact: Claude is read-only and explicitly not final approver, enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic packet/receipt handling (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). (8) CP178+ hardening: readiness auth preflight passed with 1434-byte valid JSON stdout (no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped output), and pack_level_claude_review shows no invalid attempts laundered as valid. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (LDIP invalid-attempt JSON, workbook docs, v2.0 docx) are correctly excluded from pack scope. One P3 informational note only: the final-closeout receipt artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are intentionally pending downstream of this read-only run and human final approval is still required; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Final-closeout adjudication/inspection/review-result artifacts are pending by design downstream of this read-only run
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts and the per-unit evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-476/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist yet (only manifest.json and command-evidence.json are present). This is expected, not a defect: this Claude run is step 3 (read_only_claude_run_with_raw_capture) of the 9-step hardened_review_sequence, and those artifacts plus raw-output.json / review-receipt.json are produced in the later normalize/receipt_validate/finding_adjudication/construction_inspection steps. Consistent with pack status implemented_pending_review, production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, and pack_level_claude_review.status=pending. No production_ready or enterprise-trust boolean is asserted from local validation.
findings.recommendation: At final-closeout validation, confirm claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, and review-receipt.json are generated, schema-valid, and reference this read-only run before commit; the human approver must not interpret this PASS as final approval or enterprise-trust closure.
